title: Palindrome Number
hints: Negative numbers can never be palindromes because of the sign. Also, any positive number ending in 0 (except 0 itself) is not a palindrome.
Reverse the integer mathematically by repeatedly taking the remainder modulo 10 and dividing by 10. Compare the reversed number with the original.
To avoid overflow, only reverse half of the number. If the original equals the reversed half (or reversed half divided by 10), it is a palindrome.
